origin.capability_match: Influence protocol upgrades and parameter changes

attack_surface.data_description: Interaction with the voting or proposal system that exploits a flaw to gain undue influence.

security_gap.gap_description: A vulnerability in the governance contract (e.g., vote counting, proposal validation, tokenomic interaction like flash loans for voting power) could allow a DAO participant to gain more influence than their stake or role permits.

actor_risk.description: A DAO participant might exploit a flaw to illegitimately increase their governance power, effectively gaining privileges they are not entitled to.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Unauthorized elevation of privilege in governance can lead to protocol capture, malicious changes, or theft of treasury funds.

assumptions: A flaw exists in the governance smart contract's logic related to voting power calculation, vote aggregation, or execution of proposals.
The participant has identified and can trigger this flaw.
Mechanisms like time-locks or veto powers are insufficient or can also be bypassed by the same exploit.

mitigation.strategy: Conduct comprehensive security audits and formal verification of governance contracts. Implement robust testing for all edge cases in voting power calculation and proposal execution. Use time-locks for critical changes and consider a security council with veto power for emergency situations. Ensure voting power is based on locked or time-weighted stake to prevent flash loan attacks.

description: A Superfluid DAO Participant could exploit a vulnerability in the governance mechanism (e.g., vote counting error, flash loan for temporary voting power if not mitigated) to gain undue influence over protocol upgrades or parameter changes, effectively elevating their privileges.

trace_source.matched_capability: Influence protocol upgrades and parameter changes
